note_text: Annual compliance training scheduled across all channels for Q2. Reps and agents pulled for a half day each; no material operational impact expected.
author: People Ops

note_text: CRM maintenance window Saturday night into Sunday morning. Expect a brief reporting lag Sunday; numbers will catch up by Monday.
author: IT Operations

note_text: Memorial Day holiday Monday — field crews and outbound desks dark, inbound on a skeleton shift. Lighter volume across the board for the day.
author: Operations

note_text: Spring hiring done — added two field crews and brought tenure back up after winter attrition. Running ride-alongs this week to get the new reps productive.
author: Field Ops — ERCOT North

note_text: Opened up a few new ZIPs north of the metro. Crews knocked roughly 3,800 doors last week; contact rates look fine but it's early ground so cost per knock is higher than our core turf.
author: Door Sales Ops

note_text: Pushed the door-to-door field team hard on May 6 to chase Q2 growth targets — raised commissions, added crews and hours, and stood up a short-term sales-incentive bonus. Expect elevated cost-per-acquisition in the near term, with incentive payouts landing at month-end.
author: J. Rivera, Channel Marketing

note_text: Bumped the commission tier again mid-month to keep reps motivated on the Q2 push. Reps are knocking more and writing more, but we're seeing a lot of soft yeses — cost is climbing faster than clean enrollments.
author: Door Sales Ops

note_text: Rain Friday and Saturday cost us two field days — crews are behind on the month's target and leaning on overtime to make it up. Costs keep stacking with not much extra volume to show for it.
author: Field Ops — ERCOT North

note_text: Month-end incentive spiffs paid out for the Q2 push — about $8.5k across DoorPoint and Summit crews. Planned, but it's another bump on top of an already heavy commission month.
author: Finance Operations

note_text: Finance flagged a late April field-sales commission invoice (~$9.8k overage) from DoorPoint that will post in May. April CPA may be restated once it lands.
author: Finance Operations

note_text: Late commission true-up from DoorPoint (~$4k) for end-of-May knocks posted after the books closed. Bumps closed-May spend up a touch — flagging as a restatement.
author: Finance Operations

note_text: South field channel steady. FieldForce crew tenure is high and close rates are holding where we'd expect — no changes planned this month.
author: Door Sales Ops

note_text: Paid search budget holding steady. SearchAds and BidStream both pacing to plan; nothing unusual in CPA.
author: Digital Acquisition

note_text: Refreshed ad creative on BidStream this week. Early click-through is up a little, CPA basically flat — digital spend is capped by budget so no runaway risk here.
author: Paid Media

note_text: MetroSearch added a handful of new keywords in the PECO area. Spend staying within budget; watching that the new terms don't drag efficiency.
author: Digital Acquisition

note_text: DialPro forecast revised up slightly — we're adding a small evening shift to catch more after-work answers, so expect modestly higher cost this month.
author: Telesales Ops — North

note_text: Two senior SouthDial agents gave notice this week. Backfilling now, but we'll be light on experienced closers on the South desk for a bit.
author: Contact Center Operations

note_text: High agent turnover in the outbound calling center this week. Lead quality and close rates slipping; more drop-offs than usual until we rehire and retrain.
author: Contact Center Operations

note_text: Still short-staffed on the South desk. The lead list we're working is older than usual and we're getting more hang-ups — conversion is running below where it should be.
author: Telesales Ops — South

note_text: ERCOT West territory officially launched. First telemarketing deals expected this week; no historical baseline yet, so early metrics run off plan.
author: Market Expansion

note_text: West desk is ramping. WestCall agents still learning the script and the local offers, so early numbers are off plan as expected — should tighten up over the next couple of weeks.
author: Telesales Ops — West

note_text: Direct-mail print vendor (MailWorks) renewed annual agreement; commercial terms unchanged from last year.
author: Partnerships

note_text: Spring drop went out — roughly 120k pieces in the PECO footprint. Responses trickle in over the next few weeks, so attribution this month will look light until they land.
author: Lifecycle Marketing

note_text: PrintReach drop slipped a few days on a press issue. West mail responses will lag a bit; nothing wrong with the offer, just timing.
author: Lifecycle Marketing

note_text: AffiliateHub bills monthly, so there's no in-month spend booked yet against these enrollments — CPA here will read as an estimate until the month-end invoice posts.
author: Partnerships

note_text: PartnerStream traffic steady and lead quality fine. No changes to the partner mix this month.
author: Partnerships

note_text: EastConnect reworked the IVR menu so larger commercial callers route to senior reps faster. Two sizable C&I deals in the pipeline this week off the back of it.
author: Contact Center Operations
